title: Oxalis boreaui
intro: Oxalis borbonica, also known as the wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, including its ability to treat digestive issues and its potential anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and gout. The bioactive constituents of Oxalis borbonica include oxalic acid, flavonoids, and anthocyanins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
taxonomy: Oxalis borealis, which is more commonly known as the purple wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It is native to northern parts of the world, including Europe and Asia. This species is part of the Oxalis genus, which includes around 800 species of plants. Oxalis borealis is further classified as a member of the order Rosales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is also known as the Oxalis genus of plants, which is commonly referred to as the wood sorrel.
